title: How rotating storms create some of the strongest winds on Earth

lesson.learn:
Tornadoes are violently rotating columns of air that extend from a storm cloud to the ground. They form when wind shear, instability, and storm structure line up in just the right way to create a spinning updraft. Once that rotation tightens, wind speeds can become extreme enough to strip buildings, lift vehicles, and carve out a narrow but devastating path.

What makes tornadoes especially frightening is how concentrated the damage can be. A storm might destroy one neighborhood while leaving the next street almost untouched. That is the signature of a vortex: enormous energy packed into a small, moving core.
lesson.simpler: A tornado is a spinning storm tube that reaches the ground. Its wind can be incredibly strong even though the path is narrow.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Tornadoes are dangerous because they focus huge energy into a compact rotating funnel.
lesson.deeperDive:
The physics begins with a storm that already has strong vertical motion. Add wind shear, and the rising air starts to spin. If the spin stretches into a narrower column, the rotation speeds up, much like an ice skater pulling in their arms. That tightening can create a vortex with devastating winds at the ground.

Damage is not only about wind speed. Pressure changes, flying debris, and sudden changes in direction all matter. That is why tornado safety depends on getting low, protecting the head, and staying away from windows. The storm may be brief, but the physics is brutally efficient.
lesson.funFact: A tornado's narrow path can hide how powerful it really is.
references: National Weather Service - tornado safety and science

people: Emergency managers - plan shelter and response
places: Supercell storms - the classic tornado environment
Tornado alley - a frequent U.S. region
Storm shelters - where people take cover
years: 2020s - forecasting and warning systems keep improving
concepts: Wind shear - wind changing speed or direction with height
Funnel cloud - the visible cloud tube
Enhanced Fujita scale - tornado damage rating
